text.title: SEO for SPAs — Antipatterns
text.summary: Critical failure modes: client-only content, stale ISR meta, og:image escaping, and Playwright as crawl proxy.

antipattern.description: Rendering critical SEO content (title, description, product name, article body) only on the client after hydration.
antipattern.reason: Social crawlers and many search engine bots do not execute JavaScript. Client-only content is invisible to them. The page appears blank or generic in search results and social shares.

antipattern.description: Returning HTTP 200 with an empty or "not found" UI for missing slugs instead of HTTP 404.
antipattern.reason: Search Console treats 200+empty as a soft 404 and flags it. Crawl budget is wasted on these URLs. Use notFound: true in getStaticProps or return 404 explicitly.

antipattern.description: Verifying meta tags by reading the post-hydration DOM via Playwright instead of fetching raw SSR HTML with curl.
antipattern.reason: Playwright returns the fully hydrated browser DOM, which includes client-side meta injected after JS executes. curl -A "Googlebot/2.1" fetches what the crawler actually receives.

antipattern.description: Deriving the canonical URL from window.location.hostname or request.headers.host at runtime.
antipattern.reason: Preview deployment domains (deploy-preview-123.netlify.app) become canonical URLs, suppressing the production URL in search results.

antipattern.description: Using product:price:amount instead of og:price:amount (or vice versa) for product Open Graph meta tags.
antipattern.reason: Facebook product schema uses product:price:amount. og:price:amount is not a standard property. Wrong properties silently produce no rich preview. Cross-check against ogp.me before shipping.

section.title: Sitemap lastmod Set to Build Time

antipattern.description: Setting lastmod in sitemap.xml to the build timestamp rather than the content's actual updatedAt date.
antipattern.reason: Googlebot sees that every URL changed on every deploy and re-crawls the entire site, wasting crawl budget on unchanged pages and delaying indexing of actually updated content.
